note: verse 'mahājane kuñjara mandanāga' addressing a great being; kuñjara (elephant) + mandanāga as honorific address; polysemy elephant/honorific
'tasmā mahānāga vihāya hiṃsaṃ' - mahānāga as vocative honorific of a great being in admonition verse
sucindharanāgī / nāgī offering milk-rice, dhammaruci nāgarāja giving grass; nāga-beings as donors, passing mention

claim: The bodhisatta was Atula the nāga-king, of great power, surrounded by hundreds of thousands of nāgas, who honoured the Buddha
note: atulo nāma nāgarājā hutvā; bodhisatta born as nāga-king
'nekānaṃ nāgakoṭīnaṃ parivāretvāna' - the nāga-king (Atula) with his nāga retinue going to the Buddha; passing mention of retinue
gloss of nāgakoṭīnaṃ = anekāhi nāgakoṭīhi; Atula nāga-king's nāga retinue; lexical/passing
dhanapālaka-nāga = the elephant Dhanapālaka (Nāḷāgiri); 'taming of the elephant Dhanapālaka' scene
'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā' - like an elephant breaking its bonds; simile (glossed hatthī viya in next row)
explicit gloss: 'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvāti hatthī viya pūtilatābandhanaṃ chinditvā' - nāga = elephant simile
nāgarājā in list deva/gandhabba/nāgarāja/yakkha; sace nāgarājā bhavissati pathaviyaṃ nimujjitvā gamissati - nāga-being trait, but here as identity-test option / passing
kāḷanāgarājaṃ woken; devanāgayakkhasiddhā honouring; nāga-king + list of beings, passing
Mahākāḷa nāgarājā with 80,000 nāga-dancers praising the bodhisatta; passing/scenery
Mucalinda nāgarājā coiling seven times to shelter the Buddha; (nāgalatā-dantakaṭṭha = betel toothstick, tree, secondary token) primary nāga = serpent, passing event

claim: By psychic transformation one can manifest a nāga-form (or boy-form, supaṇṇa-form); nāga listed among manifestable shapes
note: nāgavaṇṇaṃ dasseti as iddhi manifestation
devamanussanāgādayo rejoicing; nāgas in list of beings hearing the sound, passing

claim: Even animals - the nāgas Āravāḷa, Kāḷa, Apalāla, Dhanapāla, Pālileyya etc. - honoured the Buddha (tiracchānagatehipi pūjito)
note: compound names; Āravāḷa/Apalāla are nāgas, Dhanapāla/Pālileyyaka elephants - mixed but framed as tiracchānagata beings honouring Buddha; coding serpent as the named nāga-class core
nāgā supaṇṇā in list of beings (gandhabba/manussa/rakkhasa/nāgā/supaṇṇā/kinnara) seeing the Buddha; passing

claim: Long-lived nāgas, meritorious and of great power, rejoicing, pay homage to the supreme man
note: 'dīghāyukā nāgā puññavanto mahiddhiko' - asserts longevity/merit/power of nāgas
'dasanāgabalaṃ kāye' - ten-elephant strength of the Buddha's body; glossed as elephant-strength in following rows
explicit gloss dasanāgabalanti = dasachaddantanāgabalaṃ (ten Chaddanta-elephants); kāyabalaṃ hatthikulānusārena - elephant
gloss: dasanāgabala = dasachaddantanāgabala, ten elephants' strength; elephant
'mahānāgā pabhinnakilesā' - the monks like great nāgas, freed of defilements; honorific of arahant monks (polysemy elephant/sinless-one)

claim: An aspiration to Buddhahood succeeds only from the human state, not for those in nāga-birth etc., because they are ahetuka (rootless)
note: nāgajātiādīsu ṭhitānaṃ na samijjhati; soteriological ceiling on nāga-birth
'narā nāgā ca gandhabbā abhivādetvāna pakkamuṃ' - men, nāgas, gandhabbas paying respects; nāgas in list of beings, passing
nānāgandhavaṇṇāni = nānā+gandha+vaṇṇa (various scents and colours); not nāga morpheme
bandhanāgāre = bandhana-agāra (prison); na+agāra, not nāga
bandhanāgāre = prison (gloss of andughare); not nāga
'nāgāvaṭṭo ayaṃ' - is this a nāga-possession/turbulence? bhūta-yakkha-devatā list; nāga-being as cause of disturbance, passing/uncertain

claim: Those nāgas were immeasurable, unshakable, hard to assail; showing a lightning-flash they passed away, of great glory
note: 'tepi nāgā appameyyā asaṅkhobhā durāsadā' - asserts nāga powers/might
nāgabodhi = the ironwood (nāga) Bodhi-tree of Maṅgala Buddha; mattavaranāgagāmī = walking like a noble rutting elephant (secondary, simile). Primary tokens are the nāga-tree Bodhi; tree
'nāge dehi mahārāja hemakappanavāsase' - give the (caparisoned) elephants, O king; alongside chariots and horses; elephant
nānāgottā = nānā+gotta (of various clans/lineages); not nāga
'paccayaṃ nāgamāruyha' = nāgaṃ āruyha, mounting the elephant to go give gifts (Vessantara's state elephant); elephant
hatthināgaṃ + nāgamāruyha (mounting the elephant); Vessantara's elephant; elephant
'dadāhi pavaraṃ nāgaṃ sabbasetaṃ gajuttama' - give the excellent all-white elephant, best of elephants (gaja); elephant

'tassa nāgassa dānena' = the giving of that auspicious elephant (maṅgalahatthi); nāgarā here = citizens (negama nāgarā) is secondary token, but primary nāgassa = the elephant gift. Coding elephant as the nāga-referent.
mārañjanāgiri = a mountain place-name (Mārañjana-giri); string artefact of a toponym; treating as place/citizen-type non-target
bandhanāgāre/bandhanāgāraṃ = prison; not nāga
bandhanāgāro = prison (simile rājā/saṃsāra); also samannāgamo (sam-anu-āgama); not nāga
'ajja nāgasahassānaṃ' glossed anekesaṃ hatthisahassānaṃ - strength equal to a thousand elephants; Mātuposaka elephant-Jātaka; elephant
'kā nu te sā mahānāga' - O great elephant (Mātuposaka, bodhisatta as elephant tending blind mother); elephant
'muñcathetaṃ mahānāgaṃ ... sametu mātarā nāgo' - release this great elephant who supports his mother; Mātuposaka; elephant
'mutto ca bandhanā nāgo muttadāmāya kuñjaro' - the elephant freed from bonds, the tusker (kuñjaro); elephant

claim: Bhūridatta the bodhisatta-nāga, of nāga-iddhi, judges disputes in the nāga-realm, Virūpakkha's realm and Tāvatiṃsa; nāga assembly
note: Bhūridattacariyā; nāgabhavane, nāgaparisāya, nāgiddhiyā - nāga realm/powers

claim: A banished prince cohabited with a nāga-maiden (nāgamāṇavikā); Dhataraṭṭha nāga-king ruled the nāga-realm; describes nāga birth/lineage and realm
note: nāgamāṇavikā union, nāgarājā, nāgabhavane nāgarajjaṃ; birth/realm of nāgas

claim: Bhūridatta, disgusted with nāga-existence (jigucchitvā nāgattabhāvaṃ), goes to keep uposatha to win merit toward Buddhahood; nāga-maidens, nāga-realm
note: nāgattabhāve dullabhaṃ ... uposathavāsaṃ; nāga keeping uposatha to transcend nāga-birth
gloss virūpakkhena = nāgādhipati-mahārājā (lord of nāgas); identifies Virūpakkha as nāga-overlord; lexical/passing

claim: Bhūridatta keeps uposatha at the anthill, surrounded by nāga-maidens, returning to the nāga-realm; reveals himself as nāga
note: nāgakaññāhi parivāriyamānaṃ; uposathavāsa; nāga-being keeping precepts
'mahānubhāvaṃ nāgaṃ dassessāmi' - I will show you a mighty nāga (Bhūridatta to be captured); passing reference to the nāga
Alampāyana captures the nāga; nāga drops the gem and sinks into earth back to nāgabhavana; Bhūridatta as nāga, passing narrative
'nikkhama mahānāgā' / nāgassa naccaṃ - the nāga's dance; Bhūridatta made to perform; passing narrative
gloss 'eso nāgo ... vammikamatthake sayito' - this nāga lying on the anthill; locating Bhūridatta; lexical/passing
nāgabhavanato nikkhami; Accimukhī comes from nāga-realm seeking Bhūridatta; 'ehi mahānāgā'; passing narrative
Sudassana confronts Alampāyana over the captured nāga; 'ayaṃ nāgo mayhaṃ kiṃ karissati'; passing narrative

claim: Bhūridatta teaches Dhamma to the great nāga-assembly come to ask after his health, refuting Kāṇāriṭṭha, establishing them in virtue and right view
note: mahatiyā nāgaparisāya dhammaṃ desetvā sīlasampadāya diṭṭhisampadāya patiṭṭhāpetvā; nāgas hearing/practising Dhamma

claim: In his nāga-realm Bhūridatta, served by 16,000 nāga-maidens amid nāga-lordship (nāgalokissariya), without pride attended his parents and resolved disputes of the nāga-assembly
note: nāgabhavanaṭṭhāne, nāgakaññāsahassehi, nāgalokissariye, nāgaparisāya; nāga realm/sovereignty

claim: The bodhisatta, born as Campeyya the nāga-king, disgusted with the animal-birth (tiracchānayoni), keeps uposatha in the nāga-realm to fulfil the perfections
note: campānāgabhavane nāgarājā; uposathakammaṃ; nāga keeping precepts to transcend nāga-state
a youth learns the alampāyana-charm to capture the nāga (Campeyya) for display; 'imaṃ nāgaṃ gahetvā'; passing narrative
the nāga-king (Campeyya) refuses frogs/parched grain, won't eat; nāgarañño/nāgarājā; passing narrative

claim: The nāga-king made his nāga-form vanish and stood in the form of a divine youth (māṇavaka)
note: Mahāsatto nāgavaṇṇaṃ antaradhāpetvā māṇavakavaṇṇena ... aṭṭhāsi; nāga taking human form

cross-reference list: hatthināga- / bhūridatta- / campeyyanāgarāja-cariyā; campeyyanāgarāja = the Campeyya nāga-king cariyā (serpent); hatthināga (elephant cariyā) is secondary token. Primary nāga in 'campeyyanāgarāja' = serpent; passing cross-ref

note: nāgayonisiddhāhi dvīhi jivhāhi; dāṭhāvudho ghoraviso; uragādhibhū; classification/traits of nāga

claim: Hunters carry off the nāga-king Saṅkhapāla to cook and eat his flesh; Āḷāra ransoms him, noting the mighty nāga-king did them no harm by his virtue
note: nāgassa maṃsaṃ ... khādissāma; nāgarājā attano sīlaguṇena na dubbhi; nāga as prey + its virtue

'nāgañca sutvāna mamañca rāja' - having heard the nāga and me, O king; passing verse reference to Saṅkhapāla nāga

claim: As Saṅkhapāla the nāga-king of great power and fierce venom, the bodhisatta gave up his life-protection to keep virtue toward anyone; contrasts hatthināga (elephant) cariyā
note: saṅkhapālanāgarājassa ... uggavisatejassa; hatthināgacariyā (elephant) is secondary token. Primary = Saṅkhapāla serpent; soteriological/ontological
bandhanāgāre = prison (gloss of pakkhittayoghare); not nāga

'nāgabalo thāmasampanno' of Sutasoma (man) - strength-simile = elephant-strength; nāgarā (citizens) is secondary token. Primary nāgabalo = elephant-strength sense; non-claim

nānāgāhā = nānā+gāha (holding various views/grasps); not nāga

note: ambatitthako nāma nāgo āsīviso ghoraviso; venom/power of the nāga

report that Sāgata tamed the nāga spread through the land; 'nāgassa damitabhāvo'; passing reference to the tamed nāga

note: 'ghoraviso nāgo sīlavāti na haññati' - asserts a virtuous (uposatha-keeping) venomous nāga is spared; soteriological-moral claim about the nāga

claim: People make a nāga-offering (nāgabalikamma) of milk-rice, fish, meat and liquor at the seashore for the nāgas

claim: An aspiration to Buddhahood succeeds only in the human state, not for a nāga, supaṇṇa, or deity (na nāgassa ... patthanā samijjhati)

bandhanāgāre = prison; not nāga
bandhanāgāre = prison (king imprisoned, develops mettā-jhāna); not nāga

claim: Behold: a venomous nāga (serpent) is not killed because it is virtuous; virtue is supreme
note: 'ghoraviso nāgo sīlavāti na haññati' - virtuous venomous nāga spared; moral/soteriological claim (parallel to 16_p54)

claim: A nāga and a supaṇṇa watching a festival; the supaṇṇa, natural predator, pursues the terrified nāga who flees over the river; devas, nāgas, supaṇṇas attend
note: nāgo/supaṇṇo enmity; devanāgasupaṇṇādayo; nāga-supaṇṇa predation relationship

claim: The supaṇṇa explains it cannot eat the nāga-king who entered (the ascetic's) bark-garment, out of reverence; 'chief of the uragas (serpents) entered in gem-form'
note: uragānaṃ pavaro nāgarājā; supaṇṇa won't devour the nāga; nāga as supaṇṇa's prey + nāga shapeshift to gem

claim: Blessing the supaṇṇa: may divine food appear to you, do not become a nāga-flesh-eater by killing
note: 'mā ... nāgamaṃsakhādako ahosi' - exhortation not to eat nāga-flesh; nāga as the supaṇṇa's natural prey
